title: Pilosella guthnikiana
intro: Pilosella guthnikiana, also known as a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), is a rare and endangered species found in Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling, as well as support wound healing and immune function. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella guthnikiana include sesquiterpene lactones, such as erigerol and eriodictyol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pilosella guthnikiana is typically prepared as a tincture or infusion, although its use is largely limited due to its rarity and lack of scientific research.
taxonomy: Pilosella guthnikiana is a species of plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pilosella, which is a group of plants often referred to as 'mouse-ear hawk's-beard' due to their appearance. The genus Pilosella is further classified within the tribe Cichorieae, which includes a wide range of plants that are closely related to dandelions. Pilosella guthnikiana is native to the mountains of central Europe. The classification of Pilosella guthnikiana reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Asteraceae family.
